name: ipapocket
documentation:
# ipapocket

`ipapocket` is a python library for interacting with FreeIPA network protocols. `ipapocket` is focused on providing low-level programmatic access to protocols through a convenient object-oriented API, with which you can construct packets from scratch or parse them from raw data. `ipapocket` also provides several tools as an example of what can be done with this library.

## Why?

Due to lack of [support for FreeIPA](https://github.com/fortra/impacket/pull/1684#issuecomment-1986367074) features in impacket and attempts to make a more user-friendly interface for interacting with kerberos (we plan to add more network protocols in the future).

## For developers

**WARNING: This version of the codebase is under active development so the API may change over time**

Install it via `pip` form GitHub:

```sh
pip install git+https://github.com/nu11zy/ipapocket
```

Consider to use a Python virtual environment.

## For pentesters

You can install the tools from the examples using `pipx`:

```sh
pipx install git+https://github.com/nu11zy/ipapocket
```

## Examples AKA the pentest tools

`ipp-cve-2024-3183.py`: PoC for CVE-2024-3183. To crack hashes with AES256-SHA1 (etype 18) you can use this [fork of hashcat](https://github.com/nu11zy/hashcat) with mode `32900`

`ipp-id2entry.py`: parse output of `dbscan -f id2entry.db` and decrypt principals keys (can be used for creation of tickets)

`ipp-get-tgt.py`: get AS-REP and save TGT to CCACHE (normal authentication flow with PASSWORD)

`ipp-get-tgt-spake.py`: get AS-REP and save TGT to CCACHE (SPAKE hardened password)

`ipp-get-tgs.py`: get TGS-REP and save TGS to CCACHE (only use TGT from KRB5CCNAME)

`ipp-user-enum.py`: enumarate users via Kerberos

`ipp-show-ccache.py`: describe credentials in CCACHE

functions.name: examples.ipp-user-enum.main
documentation:
Example of usage:
python3 ./examples/user_enum.py -U ~/users.list -d ipa.test -H ipa.test

methods.name: examples.ipp-cve-2024-3183.Cve_2024_3183.exploit
examples.ipp-show-ccache.ShowCcache.__init__
documentation:
Read CCACHE file in such order:
1. KRB5CCNAME
2. Supplied path

methods.name: examples.ipp-get-tgt.GetTgt.get_tgt
documentation:
1. Send AS-REQ without preauth to get salt of user
2. Send AS-REQ with encrypted timestamp
3. Get salt from AS-REP (to handle case with no-preauth)
4. Save TGT

class_variables.name: examples.ipp-get-tgt.GetTgt._tgt_renewable
documentation: set RENEWABLE KDC option in AS-REQ

classes.name: ipapocket.exceptions.exceptions.Asn1ConstrainedViolation
documentation: Constrained violation of ASN1 data type

classes.name: ipapocket.exceptions.exceptions.NoSupportedEtypes
documentation: When client is not support etypes from FreeIPA server

methods.name: examples.ipp-id2entry.Id2Entry.parse
documentation: Parse LDIF dump and create list of values

methods.name: examples.ipp-id2entry.Id2Entry.dump
documentation:
Process parsed LDIF dump:
1. Extract MKey (master key) and krbPrincipalKey (blobs with principals secrets)
2. Decrypt principal secrets using master key
3. Output this

methods.name: examples.ipp-get-tgs.GetTgs.get_tgs
documentation:
1. parse CCACHE
2. create TGS-REQ packet
3. send packet and process response

class_variables.name: examples.ipp-get-tgs.GetTgs._tgt_ccache_id
documentation: ID of credential in CCACHE with TGT

class_variables.name: examples.ipp-get-tgs.GetTgs._tgs_renewable
documentation: set RENEWABLE KDC option to TGS-REQ

functions.name: ipapocket.krb5.crypto.crypto.supported_groups
documentation: List of supported SPAKE groups (by client)

functions.name: ipapocket.krb5.crypto.sp800.SP800_108_Counter
documentation:
Derive one or more keys from a master secret using
a pseudorandom function in Counter Mode, as specified in
[NIST SP 800-108r1](https://nvlpubs.nist.gov/nistpubs/SpecialPublications/NIST.SP.800-108r1.pdf).

Args:
: master (byte string):

  : The secret value used by the KDF to derive the other keys.
    It must not be a password.
    The length on the secret must be consistent with the input expected by
    the {data}`prf` function.

  key_len (integer):

  : The length in bytes of each derived key.

  prf (function):

  : A pseudorandom function that takes two byte strings as parameters:
    the secret and an input. It returns another byte string.

  num_keys (integer):

  : The number of keys to derive. Every key is {data}`key_len` bytes long.
    By default, only 1 key is derived.

  label (byte string):

  : Optional description of the purpose of the derived keys.
    It must not contain zero bytes.

  context (byte string):

  : Optional information pertaining to
    the protocol that uses the keys, such as the identity of the
    participants, nonces, session IDs, etc.
    It must not contain zero bytes.

Return:
: - a byte string (if `num_keys` is not specified), or
  - a tuple of byte strings (if `num_key` is specified).

functions.name: ipapocket.krb5.crypto.ed25519.basic.encodepoint
documentation: points are encoded as 32-bytes little-endian, b255 is sign, b2b1b0 are 0

methods.name: ipapocket.krb5.crypto.ed25519.basic.ElementOfUnknownGroup.__init__
documentation: This is used for points of order 2,4,8,2*L,4*L,8*L

methods.name: ipapocket.krb5.crypto.ed25519.basic.Element.add
documentation: this only holds elements in the main 1*L subgroup. It never holds Zero,

methods.name: ipapocket.krb5.crypto.ed25519.basic.Element.negate
documentation: negation and subtraction only make sense for the main subgroup

documentation: Validate that value in specified range
Int32           ::= INTEGER (-2147483648..2147483647) -- signed values representable in 32 bits

classes.name: ipapocket.krb5.asn1.asn1.KerberosStringsAsn1
documentation: type to store sequence of strings

classes.name: ipapocket.krb5.asn1.asn1.PrincipalNameAsn1
ipapocket.krb5.asn1.asn1.KerberosFlagsAsn1
documentation:
KerberosFlags   ::= BIT STRING (SIZE (32..MAX))
                   -- minimum number of bits shall be sent,
                   -- but no fewer than 32

classes.name: ipapocket.krb5.asn1.asn1.KdcOptionsAsn1
documentation:
KDCOptions      ::= KerberosFlags
    -- reserved(0),
    -- forwardable(1),
    -- forwarded(2),
    -- proxiable(3),
    -- proxy(4),
    -- allow-postdate(5),
    -- postdated(6),
    -- unused7(7),
    -- renewable(8),
    -- unused9(9),
    -- unused10(10),
    -- opt-hardware-auth(11),
    -- unused12(12),
    -- unused13(13),
    -- 15 is reserved for canonicalize
    -- unused15(15),
    -- 26 was unused in 1510
    -- disable-transited-check(26),
    -- renewable-ok(27),
    -- enc-tkt-in-skey(28),
    -- renew(30),
    -- validate(31)

classes.name: ipapocket.krb5.asn1.asn1.TicketFlagsAsn1
documentation:
TicketFlags     ::= KerberosFlags
    -- reserved(0),
    -- forwardable(1),
    -- forwarded(2),
    -- proxiable(3),
    -- proxy(4),
    -- may-postdate(5),
    -- postdated(6),
    -- invalid(7),
    -- renewable(8),
    -- initial(9),
    -- pre-authent(10),
    -- hw-authent(11),
    -- the following are new since 1510
    -- transited-policy-checked(12),
    -- ok-as-delegate(13)

classes.name: ipapocket.krb5.asn1.asn1.KerberosTimeAsn1
documentation: KerberosTime ::= GeneralizedTime -- with no fractional seconds

classes.name: ipapocket.krb5.asn1.asn1.EncTypesAsn1
documentation: sequence of integers to store types of encryption algos

classes.name: ipapocket.krb5.asn1.asn1.HostAddressAsn1
ipapocket.krb5.asn1.asn1.HostAddressesAsn1
documentation:
-- NOTE: HostAddresses is always used as an OPTIONAL field and
-- should not be empty.
HostAddresses   -- NOTE: subtly different from rfc1510,
               -- but has a value mapping and encodes the same
       ::= SEQUENCE OF HostAddress

classes.name: ipapocket.krb5.asn1.asn1.EncryptedDataAsn1
ipapocket.krb5.asn1.asn1.TicketAsn1
ipapocket.krb5.asn1.asn1.KdcReqBodyAsn1
documentation:
KDC-REQ-BODY    ::= SEQUENCE {
    kdc-options             [0] KDCOptions,
    cname                   [1] PrincipalName OPTIONAL
                                -- Used only in AS-REQ --,
    realm                   [2] Realm
                                -- Server's realm
                                -- Also client's in AS-REQ --,
    sname                   [3] PrincipalName OPTIONAL,
    from                    [4] KerberosTime OPTIONAL,
    till                    [5] KerberosTime,
    rtime                   [6] KerberosTime OPTIONAL,
    nonce                   [7] UInt32,
    etype                   [8] SEQUENCE OF Int32 -- EncryptionType
                                -- in preference order --,
    addresses               [9] HostAddresses OPTIONAL,
    enc-authorization-data  [10] EncryptedData OPTIONAL
                                -- AuthorizationData --,
    additional-tickets      [11] SEQUENCE OF Ticket OPTIONAL
                                    -- NOTE: not empty

classes.name: ipapocket.krb5.asn1.asn1.PaDataAsn1
ipapocket.krb5.asn1.asn1.KdcReqAsn1
documentation:
KDC-REQ         ::= SEQUENCE {
    -- NOTE: first tag is [1], not [0]
    pvno            [1] INTEGER (5) ,
    msg-type        [2] INTEGER (10 -- AS -- | 12 -- TGS --),
    padata          [3] SEQUENCE OF PA-DATA OPTIONAL
                        -- NOTE: not empty --,
    req-body        [4] KDC-REQ-BODY
}

classes.name: ipapocket.krb5.asn1.asn1.ApReqAsn1
documentation:
AP-REQ          ::= [APPLICATION 14] SEQUENCE {
       pvno            [0] INTEGER (5),
       msg-type        [1] INTEGER (14),
       ap-options      [2] APOptions,
       ticket          [3] Ticket,
       authenticator   [4] EncryptedData -- Authenticator
}

classes.name: ipapocket.krb5.asn1.asn1.EncryptionKeyAsn1
ipapocket.krb5.asn1.asn1.AuthorizationDataElementAsn1
ipapocket.krb5.asn1.asn1.ChecksumAsn1
ipapocket.krb5.asn1.asn1.AuthenticatorAsn1
documentation:
Authenticator   ::= [APPLICATION 2] SEQUENCE  {
       authenticator-vno       [0] INTEGER (5),
       crealm                  [1] Realm,
       cname                   [2] PrincipalName,
       cksum                   [3] Checksum OPTIONAL,
       cusec                   [4] Microseconds,
       ctime                   [5] KerberosTime,
       subkey                  [6] EncryptionKey OPTIONAL,
       seq-number              [7] UInt32 OPTIONAL,
       authorization-data      [8] AuthorizationData OPTIONAL
}

classes.name: ipapocket.krb5.asn1.asn1.LastReqAsn1
ipapocket.krb5.asn1.asn1.LastReqsAsn1
documentation: type to hold sequences of LastReq

classes.name: ipapocket.krb5.asn1.asn1.EncKdcRepPartAsn1
documentation:
EncKDCRepPart   ::= SEQUENCE {
    key             [0] EncryptionKey,
    last-req        [1] LastReq,
    nonce           [2] UInt32,
    key-expiration  [3] KerberosTime OPTIONAL,
    flags           [4] TicketFlags,
    authtime        [5] KerberosTime,
    starttime       [6] KerberosTime OPTIONAL,
    endtime         [7] KerberosTime,
    renew-till      [8] KerberosTime OPTIONAL,
    srealm          [9] Realm,
    sname           [10] PrincipalName,
    caddr           [11] HostAddresses OPTIONAL
}

classes.name: ipapocket.krb5.asn1.asn1.KdcRepAsn1
documentation:
KDC-REP         ::= SEQUENCE {
    pvno            [0] INTEGER (5),
    msg-type        [1] INTEGER (11 -- AS -- | 13 -- TGS --),
    padata          [2] SEQUENCE OF PA-DATA OPTIONAL
                            -- NOTE: not empty --,
    crealm          [3] Realm,
    cname           [4] PrincipalName,
    ticket          [5] Ticket,
    enc-part        [6] EncryptedData
                            -- EncASRepPart or EncTGSRepPart,
                            -- as appropriate
}

classes.name: ipapocket.krb5.asn1.asn1.KrbErrorAsn1
documentation:
KRB-ERROR       ::= [APPLICATION 30] SEQUENCE {
       pvno            [0] INTEGER (5),
       msg-type        [1] INTEGER (30),
       ctime           [2] KerberosTime OPTIONAL,
       cusec           [3] Microseconds OPTIONAL,
       stime           [4] KerberosTime,
       susec           [5] Microseconds,
       error-code      [6] Int32,
       crealm          [7] Realm OPTIONAL,
       cname           [8] PrincipalName OPTIONAL,
       realm           [9] Realm -- service realm --,
       sname           [10] PrincipalName -- service name --,
       e-text          [11] KerberosString OPTIONAL,
       e-data          [12] OCTET STRING OPTIONAL
}

    attribute-major-vno       [0] UInt16,
    attribute-minor-vno       [1] UInt16,
    kvno                      [2] UInt32,
    mkvno                     [3] UInt32 OPTIONAL,
    keys                      [4] SEQUENCE OF KrbKey,
    ...
}

EncTicketPart   ::= [APPLICATION 3] SEQUENCE {
       flags                   [0] TicketFlags,
       key                     [1] EncryptionKey,
       crealm                  [2] Realm,
       cname                   [3] PrincipalName,
       transited               [4] TransitedEncoding,
       authtime                [5] KerberosTime,
       starttime               [6] KerberosTime OPTIONAL,
       endtime                 [7] KerberosTime,
       renew-till              [8] KerberosTime OPTIONAL,
       caddr                   [9] HostAddresses OPTIONAL,
       authorization-data      [10] AuthorizationData OPTIONAL
}

classes.name: ipapocket.krb5.asn1.asn1.KrbCredInfoAsn1
documentation:
KrbCredInfo     ::= SEQUENCE {
       key             [0] EncryptionKey,
       prealm          [1] Realm OPTIONAL,
       pname           [2] PrincipalName OPTIONAL,
       flags           [3] TicketFlags OPTIONAL,
       authtime        [4] KerberosTime OPTIONAL,
       starttime       [5] KerberosTime OPTIONAL,
       endtime         [6] KerberosTime OPTIONAL,
       renew-till      [7] KerberosTime OPTIONAL,
       srealm          [8] Realm OPTIONAL,
       sname           [9] PrincipalName OPTIONAL,
       caddr           [10] HostAddresses OPTIONAL
}

classes.name: ipapocket.krb5.asn1.asn1.EncKrbCredPartAsn1
documentation:
EncKrbCredPart  ::= [APPLICATION 29] SEQUENCE {
       ticket-info     [0] SEQUENCE OF KrbCredInfo,
       nonce           [1] UInt32 OPTIONAL,
       timestamp       [2] KerberosTime OPTIONAL,
       usec            [3] Microseconds OPTIONAL,
       s-address       [4] HostAddress OPTIONAL,
       r-address       [5] HostAddress OPTIONAL
}

ipapocket.krb5.asn1.asn1.PaSpakeAsn1
documentation:
PA-SPAKE ::= CHOICE {
support     [0] SPAKESupport,
challenge   [1] SPAKEChallenge,
response    [2] SPAKEResponse,
encdata     [3] EncryptedData,
...
}

classes.name: ipapocket.krb5.crypto.backend._EtypeBaseProfile

methods.name: ipapocket.krb5.crypto.backend._EtypeBaseProfile.splitter
documentation: Function to split block on raw ciphertext and mac

Base class for different types of encryptions
All childrens must have:
    - etype: enctyprion number from RFC
    - keysize: size of key in bytes
    - seedsize: size of seed in bytes

classes.name: ipapocket.krb5.crypto.backend._EtypeRfc3961Profile
documentation: Class to implement base encryption profiles by https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc3961#section-3

class_variables.name: ipapocket.krb5.crypto.backend._EtypeRfc8009.enctype_name
documentation: Encryption type name as byte string

methods.name: ipapocket.krb5.crypto.backend._GroupBaseProfile.calc_thash
documentation:
Calculate transcript hash
1. Initialization with all zeros with length of group hashmod digest
2. Update with SPAKESupport + SPAKEChallenge (dumped ASN1)
3. Initialize with digest of p.2
4. Update with client public key

methods.name: ipapocket.krb5.credentials.ccache.Header.default
documentation:
This is default header for version 0x0504 with tag number 1.
It can represent time offset between client and KDC,
but we will set offset to 0.
